context.setting: Watching TV with TV in Front

episode_text:
day=22; meal=general tso's chicken and broccoli; setting=Watching TV with TV in Front; time_of_day=evening;
preferences: microwave_time=3 min; occlusion_relevance=minimize front occlusion; robot_speed=medium; skewering_axis=perpendicular to major axis; web_interface_confirmation=no; transfer_mode=inside mouth transfer; outside_mouth_distance=not applicable; convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transfer=speech; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_feeding=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transfer=speech; detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding=perception; detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking=autocontinue; detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping=autocontinue; retract_between_bites=yes; bite_dipping_preference=do not dip; wait_before_autocontinue_seconds=100 sec

microwave_time.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or other foods intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed cold-served fruit meals used no reheating. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods without sauces or dips THEN 1 min or 2 min depending on the meal BECAUSE personal savory meals have shown light to moderate reheating. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meal intended to be served cold THEN no microwave BECAUSE working fruit meals matched the broader cold-food pattern. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, broccoli, breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, chicken breast strips, or hash brown THEN 2 min or 3 min depending on the meal BECAUSE multiple working savory meals selected stronger reheating than personal meals. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets and vegetables THEN 2 min BECAUSE the observed social savory meal selected moderate reheating. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND fruit or dessert items intended to be served cold, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN no microwave BECAUSE observed social cold-item meals used no reheating. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken nuggets, or broccoli THEN 3 min BECAUSE TV-in-front meals consistently selected the hottest reheating level. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit or dessert items with sauce such as bananas, brownies, and chocolate sauce THEN 2 min BECAUSE a prior TV-on-right fruit/dessert meal selected moderate reheating. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, broccoli, chicken breast strips, hash brown, breaded fish bites, or roasted potatoes THEN 1 min, 2 min, or 3 min depending on the meal, with 2 min now the best-supported option in this context BECAUSE TV-on-right savory meals have included low, moderate, and high reheating, and repeated recent savory TV-on-right meals selected 2 min.

occlusion_relevance.user_tendencies: IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion or do not consider occlusion depending on the meal BECAUSE one left-laptop work meal explicitly protected the device view while a later left-laptop evening meal did not, showing mixed evidence. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE front-laptop working meals consistently protected the front view while working. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE the right-laptop working meal protected visibility on the side of the device. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion BECAUSE repeated social-right meals, including the newest one, prioritized keeping the right side visually open. IF social setting with a person on the left AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize left occlusion BECAUSE the earlier social meal prioritized keeping the left side visually open. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize front occlusion BECAUSE the earlier TV meal explicitly protected the front view. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN minimize right occlusion or do not consider occlusion depending on the meal, with minimize right occlusion now clearly better supported overall in this context BECAUSE several TV-on-right meals protected the right-side view while fewer ignored occlusion. IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN do not consider occlusion BECAUSE personal meals did not prioritize occlusion. Overall, occlusion concern is selective and usually tracks the location of a socially or visually important target, while personal meals generally ignore occlusion.

robot_speed.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without sauces or dips THEN slow or medium depending on the meal, with slow still the better-supported personal default BECAUSE personal meals have included both cautious and somewhat faster operation. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods or fruit meals, including strawberries with whipped cream, THEN slow or medium depending on the meal, with slow still slightly better supported overall but medium now clearly possible in social settings BECAUSE earlier social meals selected slow while the newest social-right fruit meal selected medium. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods with dip available THEN slow BECAUSE the earlier TV meal used the slowest speed. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND fruit or dessert foods such as bananas and brownies with chocolate sauce THEN medium BECAUSE the prior TV-on-right fruit/dessert meal used medium speed. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken nuggets, broccoli, chicken breast strips, hash brown, breaded fish bites, or roasted potatoes THEN medium, slow, or fast depending on the meal, with medium now the clearest tendency in this context BECAUSE TV-on-right savory meals have included repeated medium selections, one slow selection, and one fast selection. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meal intended to be served cold THEN slow BECAUSE the right-laptop fruit meal matched the comfort-oriented pace seen in several other work meals. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken breast strips, hash brown, chicken nuggets, broccoli, bite-sized sandwiches, breaded fish bites, or roasted potatoes THEN medium or slow depending on the meal, with medium repeatedly supported and fast reserved for occasional efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts vary between comfort and efficiency more than other settings. Overall, the user generally prefers a cautious pace, but working and TV-on-right meals often shift toward medium-speed efficiency.

skewering_axis.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND solid bite-sized foods in general THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE observed personal meals used the reliability-oriented option. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, cut fruit, or strawberries THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE all observed social meals selected the reliability-oriented option. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND foods such as buffalo chicken bites, potato wedges, chicken nuggets, broccoli, bananas, brownies, breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, chicken breast strips, or hash brown THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE most TV meals used the reliability-oriented option. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND elongated or mouth-opening-sensitive foods such as bite-sized sandwiches THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE repeated TV-on-right sandwich meals selected the narrower-bite option. IF working context AND neutral affect AND elongated or mouth-opening-sensitive foods such as bite-sized sandwiches, fruit pieces, or chicken breast strips THEN parallel to major axis BECAUSE multiple working meals selected the narrower-bite option for foods where bite shape may matter. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken nuggets, broccoli, breaded fish bites, or roasted potatoes THEN perpendicular to major axis BECAUSE several working savory meals matched the broader reliability-oriented pattern. Overall, the user generally prefers perpendicular skewering, but food-geometry-based exceptions favor parallel skewering for narrower bites.

web_interface_confirmation.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE observed personal meals avoided extra confirmation, consistent with minimizing interaction burden. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE working meals continued to skip confirmation while occupied. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE social meals, including the newest one, avoided extra confirmation. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE TV meals also skipped confirmation. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

transfer_mode.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN inside mouth transfer BECAUSE the user cannot lean forward due to lack of trunk control, making outside-mouth transfer physically unsuitable.

outside_mouth_distance.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN not applicable BECAUSE the user's physical abilities support inside-mouth transfer rather than outside-mouth transfer.

convey_robot_ready_for_initiating_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed personal meals used multimodal signaling. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED or LED depending on the meal, with multimodal cueing still better supported overall but visual-only cueing now newly supported in social-right dining BECAUSE earlier social meals used speech + LED while the newest social-right meal used LED only. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE TV-in-front meals used multimodal signaling, consistent with wanting salient cues when attention is partly directed elsewhere. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech or speech + LED depending on the meal, with speech now slightly better supported than before but multimodal cueing still common BECAUSE TV-on-right meals have included both options. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED or speech depending on the meal, with speech + LED more common overall but speech-only appearing in some efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working meals are usually multimodal but sometimes simplify cues during more automated operation. Overall, multimodal cueing remains the stable preference, with simplified single-modality cueing appearing in some more automation-oriented contexts.

detect_user_ready_for_initiating_transfe.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the user has good mouth control and selected this low-effort, user-driven method in personal settings. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth or autocontinue depending on the meal, with evidence now mixed and a tie-break toward reduced effort/automation in some social meals BECAUSE earlier social meals used intentional mouth-based initiation while the newest social-right meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used intentional mouth-based initiation. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue or open mouth depending on the meal, with autocontinue now slightly better supported in this context BECAUSE repeated TV-on-right meals have selected both options. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth or autocontinue depending on the meal, with open mouth common across many work meals but autocontinue appearing in some efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts show the clearest tradeoff between direct control and reduced interaction burden. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN open mouth or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use; observed selections also avoid button input.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE observed personal meals used this low-effort but intentional readiness signal for drinking. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth or autocontinue depending on the meal, with evidence now mixed and a tie-break toward reduced effort/automation in some social meals BECAUSE earlier social meals used intentional mouth-based sip initiation while the newest social-right meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used intentional mouth-based sip initiation. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue or open mouth depending on the meal, with autocontinue now slightly better supported in this context BECAUSE repeated TV-on-right meals have selected both options. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN open mouth or autocontinue depending on the meal, with open mouth common across many work meals but autocontinue appearing in some efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts show the clearest shift toward automated flow. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN open mouth or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use; observed selections also avoid button input.

IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE observed personal meals used automation for wiping initiation. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE working meals also used automation. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE all observed social meals did the same, including the newest one. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN autocontinue BECAUSE both TV-in-front and TV-on-right meals also used automation. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use. No context-dependent variation observed yet.

convey_robot_ready_for_completing_transf.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE observed personal meals used multimodal completion cues. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED or LED depending on the meal, with multimodal completion cueing still better supported overall but visual-only cueing now newly supported in social-right dining BECAUSE earlier social meals used speech + LED while the newest social-right meal used LED only. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED BECAUSE TV-in-front meals used multimodal completion cues, consistent with wanting salient signals when attention is partly elsewhere. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech or speech + LED depending on the meal, with speech now slightly better supported than before but multimodal completion cueing still common BECAUSE TV-on-right meals have included both options. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN speech + LED or speech depending on the meal, with speech + LED more common overall but speech-only appearing in some efficiency-oriented work meals BECAUSE working meals are usually multimodal but sometimes simplify cues during efficiency-oriented operation. Overall, multimodal completion cueing is still the dominant preference, with simplified single-modality cueing appearing mainly in TV-on-right and some social-right meals.

detect_user_completed_transfer_feeding.user_tendencies: IF any dining context AND any affective state AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the user consistently selected reliable sensor-based completion detection for feeding, and button input is incompatible with severe upper-limb paralysis.

detect_user_completed_transfer_drinking.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE observed personal meals used perception-based drink completion and the user has good head control. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with evidence now mixed rather than clearly favoring perception BECAUSE earlier social meals used perception while the newest social-right meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used perception-based drink completion. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with evidence balanced rather than clearly favoring perception BECAUSE TV-on-right meals include both options. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with perception common across many work meals but autocontinue appearing in some automation-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts show the clearest shift toward reduced user signaling. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN perception or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use.

detect_user_completed_transfer_wiping.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE observed personal meals used perception-based wiping completion and the user has good head control. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with evidence now mixed rather than clearly favoring perception BECAUSE earlier social meals used perception while the newest social-right meal used autocontinue. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception BECAUSE the earlier TV meal also used perception-based wiping completion. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with evidence balanced rather than clearly favoring perception BECAUSE TV-on-right meals include both options. IF working context AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN perception or autocontinue depending on the meal, with perception common across many work meals but autocontinue appearing in some automation-oriented work meals BECAUSE working contexts show the clearest shift toward reduced user signaling. IF any context AND any affective state AND button input would be required THEN perception or autocontinue rather than button BECAUSE severe upper-limb paralysis prevents physical button use.

retract_between_bites.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE observed personal meals kept the robot in place between tasks to reduce meal time. IF working on a laptop or tablet positioned in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN yes BECAUSE a front-laptop evening work meal selected retracting, consistent with protecting the front workspace and screen between tasks. IF working context in other observed laptop positions AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no BECAUSE other working meals consistently prioritized efficiency over reducing visual obstruction. IF social setting with a person on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN yes or no depending on the meal, with evidence now mixed in this context BECAUSE earlier social meals skipped retracting while the newest social-right meal selected retracting alongside right-side occlusion protection. IF social setting more generally AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no remains the broader social tendency BECAUSE earlier social meals still preferred not retracting. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN yes BECAUSE the earlier TV-in-front meal selected retracting, consistent with protecting the user's front view of the screen between tasks. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN no or yes depending on the meal, with no still more common but yes supported in this context BECAUSE most prior TV-on-right meals skipped retracting for efficiency, while a later TV-on-right meal selected retracting alongside right-side occlusion protection. Overall, the user usually prioritizes shorter meal flow, but accepts retracting when protecting an important visual or social target seems especially valuable.

bite_dipping_preference.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE observed personal meals without sauces used no dipping. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND fruit meal or foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE the observed social fruit meal without a clear dip option used no dipping. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces, toppings, or dips such as ketchup or whipped cream THEN less BECAUSE social meals with an available coating selected reduced dipping/application, consistent with allowing flavor while limiting mess in front of another person. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meals or foods without dippable items or sauces THEN do not dip BECAUSE working fruit meals matched the broader no-dip pattern in work settings. IF working context AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ketchup or tartar sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE working meals with available sauces avoided dipping, consistent with reducing mess while occupied. IF watching TV context AND neutral affect AND foods include sauces or dips such as ranch dressing, ketchup, tartar sauce, or chocolate sauce THEN do not dip BECAUSE TV meals avoided dipping, suggesting the user prefers cleaner, lower-mess operation when attention is divided. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND foods without dippable items or sauces such as bite-sized sandwiches, chicken breast strips, or hash brown THEN do not dip BECAUSE repeated TV-on-right savory meals also selected no dipping. Overall, the user strongly prefers avoiding dipping except for a limited social-context reduction to less when sauce-like accompaniments are available.

wait_before_autocontinue_seconds.user_tendencies: IF quiet personal setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec or 10 sec depending on the meal, with 100 sec still the better-supported personal default but 10 sec also supported in a personal evening savory meal BECAUSE personal meals have shown both a longer response window and a shorter efficiency-oriented wait. IF social setting AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE all observed social meals, including the newest one, used the longer wait, consistent with allowing more time when attention may be shared. IF watching TV in front AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec BECAUSE the TV-in-front meal used the longer wait, consistent with allowing time when attention is partly on the screen. IF watching TV with the TV on the right AND neutral affect AND any food condition THEN 100 sec or 10 sec depending on the meal, with evidence balanced in this context BECAUSE TV-on-right meals include both settings. IF working context AND neutral affect AND fruit meals THEN 100 sec BECAUSE observed working fruit meals supported a longer response window. IF working context AND neutral affect AND warm savory foods such as chicken breast strips, hash brown, breaded fish bites, roasted potatoes, or some other work meals with more automation-oriented interaction THEN 10 sec or 100 sec depending on the meal BECAUSE working meals show mixed evidence between faster flow and a longer response window. IF working context AND neutral affect AND many other warm savory meals THEN 100 sec BECAUSE several work meals still used the longer wait. Overall, wait-time preference is stable at 100 sec outside efficiency-oriented contexts, while working meals and TV-on-right meals show the most variability.
